results.artifact: RCA report: inconclusive. SQL Lab could not be used in this run because the preliminary read-only probe returned HTTP 401 Missing Authorization Header, so I did not execute the drill queries or assert numeric contributors. The intended analysis compares March 1996 against the average of February and April 1996 by region, transaction type, and k_symbol. Based only on the task note, March is not a strong row-count outlier, so a true drop in total amount would most likely come from lower average amount per transaction rather than fewer transactions.

sql.month_summary: SELECT date_trunc('month', CAST(t.date AS DATE)) AS month, COUNT(*) AS txn_count, SUM(t.amount) AS total_amount, AVG(t.amount) AS avg_amount FROM trans t WHERE CAST(t.date AS DATE) >= DATE '1996-02-01' AND CAST(t.date AS DATE) < DATE '1996-05-01' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;
sql.region_drill: WITH m AS (SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(t.date AS DATE) >= DATE '1996-03-01' AND CAST(t.date AS DATE) < DATE '1996-04-01' THEN 'march' ELSE 'neighbor' END AS period, d."A3" AS region, SUM(t.amount) AS amount FROM trans t JOIN account a ON a.account_id=t.account_id JOIN district d ON d.district_id=a.district_id WHERE CAST(t.date AS DATE) >= DATE '1996-02-01' AND CAST(t.date AS DATE) < DATE '1996-05-01' GROUP BY 1,2) SELECT region, SUM(CASE WHEN period='march' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS march_amount, SUM(CASE WHEN period='neighbor' THEN amount ELSE 0 END)/2.0 AS neighbor_avg_amount, SUM(CASE WHEN period='march' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) - SUM(CASE WHEN period='neighbor' THEN amount ELSE 0 END)/2.0 AS gap FROM m GROUP BY region ORDER BY gap ASC;
sql.type_k_symbol_drill: WITH m AS (SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(t.date AS DATE) >= DATE '1996-03-01' AND CAST(t.date AS DATE) < DATE '1996-04-01' THEN 'march' ELSE 'neighbor' END AS period, t.type, COALESCE(t.k_symbol,'(blank)') AS k_symbol, SUM(t.amount) AS amount FROM trans t WHERE CAST(t.date AS DATE) >= DATE '1996-02-01' AND CAST(t.date AS DATE) < DATE '1996-05-01' GROUP BY 1,2,3) SELECT type, k_symbol, SUM(CASE WHEN period='march' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS march_amount, SUM(CASE WHEN period='neighbor' THEN amount ELSE 0 END)/2.0 AS neighbor_avg_amount, SUM(CASE WHEN period='march' THEN amount ELSE 0 END)-SUM(CASE WHEN period='neighbor' THEN amount ELSE 0 END)/2.0 AS gap FROM m GROUP BY type,k_symbol ORDER BY gap ASC;

results.artifact: RCA report: inconclusive. The required Q2 versus Q3 1996 loan-default drill was not executed because SQL Lab was unavailable. The planned root-cause test computes the default-rate gap first, then compares whether the increase is explained by duration mix, loan-amount bucket, geography, or gender. Without result rows, no primary driver is claimed.

sql.quarter_summary: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(date AS DATE) >= DATE '1996-04-01' AND CAST(date AS DATE) < DATE '1996-07-01' THEN '1996Q2' WHEN CAST(date AS DATE) >= DATE '1996-07-01' AND CAST(date AS DATE) < DATE '1996-10-01' THEN '1996Q3' END AS quarter, COUNT(*) AS finished_loans, SUM(CASE WHEN status='B' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS defaults, SUM(CASE WHEN status='B' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::double precision / COUNT(*) AS default_rate, AVG(amount) AS avg_amount, AVG(duration) AS avg_duration FROM loan WHERE status IN ('A','B') AND CAST(date AS DATE) >= DATE '1996-04-01' AND CAST(date AS DATE) < DATE '1996-10-01' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;
sql.duration_drill: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(l.date AS DATE) < DATE '1996-07-01' THEN '1996Q2' ELSE '1996Q3' END AS quarter, l.duration, COUNT(*) AS n, SUM(CASE WHEN l.status='B' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::double precision/COUNT(*) AS default_rate FROM loan l WHERE l.status IN ('A','B') AND CAST(l.date AS DATE) >= DATE '1996-04-01' AND CAST(l.date AS DATE) < DATE '1996-10-01' GROUP BY 1,2 ORDER BY 1,2;
sql.amount_region_gender_drill: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(l.date AS DATE) < DATE '1996-07-01' THEN '1996Q2' ELSE '1996Q3' END AS quarter, NTILE(4) OVER (PARTITION BY CASE WHEN CAST(l.date AS DATE) < DATE '1996-07-01' THEN '1996Q2' ELSE '1996Q3' END ORDER BY l.amount) AS amount_quartile, d."A3" AS region, c.gender, COUNT(*) AS n, SUM(CASE WHEN l.status='B' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::double precision/COUNT(*) AS default_rate FROM loan l JOIN account a ON a.account_id=l.account_id JOIN disp dp ON dp.account_id=a.account_id JOIN client c ON c.client_id=dp.client_id JOIN district d ON d.district_id=a.district_id WHERE dp.type='OWNER' AND l.status IN ('A','B') AND CAST(l.date AS DATE) >= DATE '1996-04-01' AND CAST(l.date AS DATE) < DATE '1996-10-01' GROUP BY 1,d."A3",c.gender,l.amount ORDER BY quarter,n DESC;

results.artifact: RCA report: partial from prompt facts only. January 1998 card-withdrawal count is stated as 1,702 versus a 1997 monthly average of 180.5, about 9.4x higher. SQL execution was unavailable, so I cannot determine whether late-1997 issuance, card type, or district concentration is the actual driver. The planned drill separates card type, district, and Q4-1997 issuance cohort shares within January 1998 card withdrawals.

sql.monthly_counts: SELECT date_trunc('month', CAST(t.date AS DATE)) AS month, COUNT(*) AS card_withdrawals FROM trans t WHERE t.operation='VYBER KARTOU' AND CAST(t.date AS DATE) >= DATE '1997-01-01' AND CAST(t.date AS DATE) < DATE '1998-02-01' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;
sql.jan1998_card_type: SELECT c.type AS card_type, COUNT(*) AS jan1998_count, COUNT(*)::double precision / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER () AS share FROM trans t JOIN disp dp ON dp.account_id=t.account_id JOIN card c ON c.disp_id=dp.disp_id WHERE t.operation='VYBER KARTOU' AND CAST(t.date AS DATE) >= DATE '1998-01-01' AND CAST(t.date AS DATE) < DATE '1998-02-01' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY jan1998_count DESC;
sql.jan1998_district_cohort: SELECT d."A2" AS district, d."A3" AS region, CASE WHEN CAST(c.issued AS DATE) >= DATE '1997-10-01' AND CAST(c.issued AS DATE) < DATE '1998-01-01' THEN 'issued_1997Q4' ELSE 'older_card' END AS issuance_cohort, COUNT(*) AS jan1998_count FROM trans t JOIN account a ON a.account_id=t.account_id JOIN district d ON d.district_id=a.district_id JOIN disp dp ON dp.account_id=a.account_id JOIN card c ON c.disp_id=dp.disp_id WHERE t.operation='VYBER KARTOU' AND CAST(t.date AS DATE) >= DATE '1998-01-01' AND CAST(t.date AS DATE) < DATE '1998-02-01' GROUP BY 1,2,3 ORDER BY jan1998_count DESC LIMIT 30;

results.artifact: RCA report: inconclusive. I could not execute the Kolin 1995 versus 1996 balance drill. The intended analysis measures the average post-transaction balance gap in district 5, then ranks changes by account frequency, owner age band, and k_symbol. No main contributor is asserted without rows.

sql.year_summary: SELECT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(t.date AS DATE)) AS year, COUNT(*) AS txn_count, AVG(t.balance) AS avg_post_txn_balance FROM trans t JOIN account a ON a.account_id=t.account_id WHERE a.district_id=5 AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(t.date AS DATE)) IN (1995,1996) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;
sql.frequency_drill: SELECT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(t.date AS DATE)) AS year, a.frequency, COUNT(*) AS txn_count, AVG(t.balance) AS avg_balance FROM trans t JOIN account a ON a.account_id=t.account_id WHERE a.district_id=5 AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(t.date AS DATE)) IN (1995,1996) GROUP BY 1,2 ORDER BY 2,1;
sql.age_k_symbol_drill: WITH owner_age AS (SELECT a.account_id, CASE WHEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM AGE(DATE '1996-12-31', c.birth_date)) < 30 THEN '<30' WHEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM AGE(DATE '1996-12-31', c.birth_date)) < 45 THEN '30-44' WHEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM AGE(DATE '1996-12-31', c.birth_date)) < 60 THEN '45-59' ELSE '60+' END AS age_band FROM account a JOIN disp dp ON dp.account_id=a.account_id AND dp.type='OWNER' JOIN client c ON c.client_id=dp.client_id WHERE a.district_id=5) SELECT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(t.date AS DATE)) AS year, oa.age_band, COALESCE(t.k_symbol,'(blank)') AS k_symbol, COUNT(*) AS txn_count, AVG(t.balance) AS avg_balance FROM trans t JOIN owner_age oa ON oa.account_id=t.account_id WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(t.date AS DATE)) IN (1995,1996) GROUP BY 1,2,3 ORDER BY 2,3,1;

results.artifact: RCA report: inconclusive. The 1997 weekly-issuance default-rate claim was not tested against data. The planned RCA compares default rates by frequency and then checks whether weekly accounts differ in district salary quartile, loan amount quartile, or duration bucket. Because these are observational cuts, any driver should be framed as associated rather than causal.

sql.frequency_rates: SELECT a.frequency, COUNT(*) AS finished_loans, SUM(CASE WHEN l.status='B' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS defaults, SUM(CASE WHEN l.status='B' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::double precision/COUNT(*) AS default_rate, AVG(l.amount) AS avg_amount, AVG(l.duration) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(d."A11" AS numeric)) AS avg_salary FROM loan l JOIN account a ON a.account_id=l.account_id JOIN district d ON d.district_id=a.district_id WHERE l.status IN ('A','B') AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(l.date AS DATE))=1997 GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY default_rate DESC;
sql.quartile_drill: WITH base AS (SELECT a.frequency, l.status, l.amount, l.duration, CAST(d."A11" AS numeric) AS salary, NTILE(4) OVER (ORDER BY CAST(d."A11" AS numeric)) AS salary_quartile, NTILE(4) OVER (ORDER BY l.amount) AS amount_quartile FROM loan l JOIN account a ON a.account_id=l.account_id JOIN district d ON d.district_id=a.district_id WHERE l.status IN ('A','B') AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(l.date AS DATE))=1997) SELECT frequency, salary_quartile, amount_quartile, CASE WHEN duration=12 THEN '12' WHEN duration IN (24,36) THEN '24-36' ELSE '48-60' END AS duration_bucket, COUNT(*) AS n, SUM(CASE WHEN status='B' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::double precision/COUNT(*) AS default_rate FROM base GROUP BY 1,2,3,4 ORDER BY frequency,n DESC;

results.artifact: RCA report: inconclusive. I could not execute the district SIPO-share outlier query. The intended result ranks districts by absolute deviation from the national 1996 SIPO transaction-count share, then profiles the top outlier using region, salary A11, and unemployment A12/A13. No district is named without query output.

sql.national_and_district: WITH district_share AS (SELECT d.district_id, d."A2" AS district, d."A3" AS region, CAST(d."A11" AS numeric) AS avg_salary, CAST(d."A12" AS numeric) AS unemployment_1995, CAST(d."A13" AS numeric) AS unemployment_1996, COUNT(*) AS txn_count, SUM(CASE WHEN t.k_symbol='SIPO' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS sipo_count, SUM(CASE WHEN t.k_symbol='SIPO' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::double precision/COUNT(*) AS sipo_share FROM trans t JOIN account a ON a.account_id=t.account_id JOIN district d ON d.district_id=a.district_id WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(t.date AS DATE))=1996 GROUP BY 1,2,3,4,5,6), national AS (SELECT SUM(sipo_count)::double precision/SUM(txn_count) AS national_sipo_share FROM district_share) SELECT ds.*, n.national_sipo_share, ds.sipo_share-n.national_sipo_share AS deviation, ABS(ds.sipo_share-n.national_sipo_share) AS abs_deviation FROM district_share ds CROSS JOIN national n ORDER BY abs_deviation DESC LIMIT 10;

results.artifact: RCA report: inconclusive. SQL was not executed, so I cannot distinguish which districts, frequencies, or regions explain the February 1994 drop in new account openings. The planned analysis treats January as a possible one-time spike rather than assuming February is abnormal, and compares account openings by district, frequency, and region for January versus February.

sql.month_counts: SELECT date_trunc('month', CAST(a.date AS DATE)) AS month, COUNT(*) AS new_accounts FROM account a WHERE CAST(a.date AS DATE) >= DATE '1994-01-01' AND CAST(a.date AS DATE) < DATE '1994-03-01' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;
sql.district_frequency_region: WITH base AS (SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(a.date AS DATE) < DATE '1994-02-01' THEN '1994-01' ELSE '1994-02' END AS month, d."A2" AS district, d."A3" AS region, a.frequency, COUNT(*) AS new_accounts FROM account a JOIN district d ON d.district_id=a.district_id WHERE CAST(a.date AS DATE) >= DATE '1994-01-01' AND CAST(a.date AS DATE) < DATE '1994-03-01' GROUP BY 1,2,3,4) SELECT district, region, frequency, SUM(CASE WHEN month='1994-01' THEN new_accounts ELSE 0 END) AS jan_accounts, SUM(CASE WHEN month='1994-02' THEN new_accounts ELSE 0 END) AS feb_accounts, SUM(CASE WHEN month='1994-02' THEN new_accounts ELSE 0 END) - SUM(CASE WHEN month='1994-01' THEN new_accounts ELSE 0 END) AS change FROM base GROUP BY 1,2,3 ORDER BY change ASC;

results.artifact: RCA report: inconclusive. The gold-versus-junior finished-loan default-rate gap and decomposition were not executed. The intended decomposition compares raw default rates, then reweights by client age band and loan-amount band to estimate how much of the gap is explained by composition. Any remaining gap should be reported as unexplained association, not causal effect.

sql.raw_gap: WITH base AS (SELECT c.type AS card_type, l.status, l.amount, cl.birth_date FROM loan l JOIN account a ON a.account_id=l.account_id JOIN disp dp ON dp.account_id=a.account_id JOIN card c ON c.disp_id=dp.disp_id JOIN client cl ON cl.client_id=dp.client_id WHERE l.status IN ('A','B') AND c.type IN ('gold','junior')) SELECT card_type, COUNT(*) AS finished_loans, SUM(CASE WHEN status='B' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS defaults, SUM(CASE WHEN status='B' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::double precision/COUNT(*) AS default_rate, AVG(amount) AS avg_amount FROM base GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY default_rate DESC;
sql.age_amount_bands: WITH base AS (SELECT c.type AS card_type, l.status, CASE WHEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM AGE(CAST(l.date AS DATE), cl.birth_date)) < 30 THEN '<30' WHEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM AGE(CAST(l.date AS DATE), cl.birth_date)) < 45 THEN '30-44' WHEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM AGE(CAST(l.date AS DATE), cl.birth_date)) < 60 THEN '45-59' ELSE '60+' END AS age_band, NTILE(4) OVER (ORDER BY l.amount) AS amount_band FROM loan l JOIN account a ON a.account_id=l.account_id JOIN disp dp ON dp.account_id=a.account_id JOIN card c ON c.disp_id=dp.disp_id JOIN client cl ON cl.client_id=dp.client_id WHERE l.status IN ('A','B') AND c.type IN ('gold','junior')) SELECT card_type, age_band, amount_band, COUNT(*) AS n, SUM(CASE WHEN status='B' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::double precision/COUNT(*) AS default_rate FROM base GROUP BY 1,2,3 ORDER BY 1,2,3;

results.artifact: RCA report: inconclusive. I could not execute the east Bohemia UVER-growth decomposition. The planned analysis compares 1996 to 1997 UVER outflow by region, then decomposes regional growth into number of loan-holding accounts or new loans, average loan size, and repayment transaction frequency. No underlying driver is asserted without rows.

sql.region_uver_growth: SELECT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(t.date AS DATE)) AS year, d."A3" AS region, SUM(t.amount) AS uver_amount, COUNT(*) AS uver_txn_count, COUNT(DISTINCT t.account_id) AS loan_payment_accounts FROM trans t JOIN account a ON a.account_id=t.account_id JOIN district d ON d.district_id=a.district_id WHERE t.k_symbol='UVER' AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(t.date AS DATE)) IN (1996,1997) GROUP BY 1,2 ORDER BY region,year;
sql.loan_driver_decomposition: SELECT d."A3" AS region, EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(l.date AS DATE)) AS approval_year, COUNT(*) AS new_loans, AVG(l.amount) AS avg_loan_amount, AVG(l.payments) AS avg_payment, AVG(l.duration) AS avg_duration FROM loan l JOIN account a ON a.account_id=l.account_id JOIN district d ON d.district_id=a.district_id WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(l.date AS DATE)) IN (1996,1997) GROUP BY 1,2 ORDER BY region,approval_year;
sql.payment_frequency: SELECT d."A3" AS region, EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(t.date AS DATE)) AS year, COUNT(*)::double precision / COUNT(DISTINCT t.account_id) AS uver_txns_per_account, AVG(t.amount) AS avg_uver_txn_amount FROM trans t JOIN account a ON a.account_id=t.account_id JOIN district d ON d.district_id=a.district_id WHERE t.k_symbol='UVER' AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(t.date AS DATE)) IN (1996,1997) GROUP BY 1,2 ORDER BY region,year;

results.artifact: RCA report: inconclusive. The status-D district concentration query was not executed, so no top-three districts or feature comparison is claimed. The intended RCA ranks districts by count of running-in-debt loans, compares the top three to the bottom three, and reports whether unemployment A13, crime A16, or salary A11 differ descriptively from low-D districts.

sql.rank_districts: WITH district_d AS (SELECT d.district_id, d."A2" AS district, d."A3" AS region, CAST(d."A11" AS numeric) AS avg_salary, CAST(d."A13" AS numeric) AS unemployment_1996, CAST(d."A16" AS numeric) AS crime_rate, COUNT(l.loan_id) FILTER (WHERE l.status='D') AS status_d_count FROM district d LEFT JOIN account a ON a.district_id=d.district_id LEFT JOIN loan l ON l.account_id=a.account_id GROUP BY 1,2,3,4,5,6), ranked AS (SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY status_d_count DESC, district_id) AS high_rank, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY status_d_count ASC, district_id) AS low_rank FROM district_d) SELECT CASE WHEN high_rank<=3 THEN 'top_3_D' WHEN low_rank<=3 THEN 'bottom_3_D' END AS comparison_group, * FROM ranked WHERE high_rank<=3 OR low_rank<=3 ORDER BY comparison_group DESC, status_d_count DESC;
sql.feature_summary: WITH district_d AS (SELECT d.district_id, CAST(d."A11" AS numeric) AS avg_salary, CAST(d."A13" AS numeric) AS unemployment_1996, CAST(d."A16" AS numeric) AS crime_rate, COUNT(l.loan_id) FILTER (WHERE l.status='D') AS status_d_count FROM district d LEFT JOIN account a ON a.district_id=d.district_id LEFT JOIN loan l ON l.account_id=a.account_id GROUP BY 1,2,3,4), ranked AS (SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY status_d_count DESC, district_id) AS high_rank, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY status_d_count ASC, district_id) AS low_rank FROM district_d) SELECT CASE WHEN high_rank<=3 THEN 'top_3_D' WHEN low_rank<=3 THEN 'bottom_3_D' ELSE 'middle' END AS group_name, COUNT(*) AS districts, AVG(status_d_count) AS avg_status_d_count, AVG(avg_salary) AS avg_salary, AVG(unemployment_1996) AS avg_unemployment_1996, AVG(crime_rate) AS avg_crime_rate FROM ranked GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;
